heading: Interests in certain financial assets of Iran

children.label: A
children.text: held by or for a foreign securities intermediary doing business in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: a blocked asset (whether or not subsequently unblocked), or an asset that would be blocked if the asset were located in the United States, that is property described in subsection (b); and

children.label: C
children.text: equal in value to a financial asset of Iran, including an asset of the central bank or monetary authority of the Government of Iran or any agency or instrumentality of that Government, that such foreign securities intermediary or a related intermediary holds abroad,

children.label: A
children.text: equitable title to, or a beneficial interest in, the assets described in subsection (b) (excluding a custodial interest of a foreign securities intermediary or a related intermediary that holds the assets abroad for the benefit of Iran); or

children.label: B
children.text: a constitutionally protected interest in the assets described in subsection (b),

identified in and the subject of proceedings in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York in Peterson et al. v. Islamic Republic of Iran et al., Case No. 10 Civ. 4518 (BSJ) (GWG), that were restrained by restraining notices and levies secured by the plaintiffs in those proceedings, as modified by court order dated , and extended by court orders dated , , and , so long as such assets remain restrained by court order; andJune 27, 2008June 23, 2009May 10, 2010June 11, 2010

identified in and the subject of proceedings in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York in Peterson et al. v. Islamic Republic of Iran et al., Case No. 13 Civ. 9195 (LAP).
to affect the availability, or lack thereof, of a right to satisfy a judgment in any other action against a terrorist party in any proceedings other than proceedings referred to in subsection (b); or
to apply to assets other than the assets described in subsection (b), or to preempt State law, including the Uniform Commercial Code, except as expressly provided in subsection (a)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: means any asset seized or frozen by the United States under or under section 202 or 203 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( and 1702); andsection 4305(b) of title 5050 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: i
children.text: is subject to a license issued by the United States Government for final payment, transfer, or disposition by or to a person subject to the jurisdiction of the United States in connection with a transaction for which the issuance of the license has been specifically required by a provision of law other than the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.) or the United Nations Participation Act of 1945 ( et seq.); or50 U.S.C. 170122 U.S.C. 287

children.label: ii
children.text: is property subject to the Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations or the Vienna Convention on Consular Relations, or that enjoys equivalent privileges and immunities under the laws of the United States, and is being used exclusively for diplomatic or consular purposes.

children.heading: Financial asset; securities intermediary
children.label: 2 Financial asset; securities intermediary
children.text: The terms “financial asset” and “securities intermediary” have the meanings given those terms in the Uniform Commercial Code, but the former includes cash.

children.heading: Iran
children.label: 3 Iran
children.text: The term “Iran” means the Government of Iran, including the central bank or monetary authority of that Government and any agency or instrumentality of that Government.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “person” means an individual or entity.

children.heading: Entity
children.label: B Entity
children.text: The term “entity” means a partnership, association, trust, joint venture, corporation, group, subgroup, or other organization.

children.heading: Terrorist party
children.label: 5 Terrorist party
children.text: The term “terrorist party” has the meaning given that term in section 201(d) of the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 ( note).28 U.S.C. 1610

children.heading: United States
children.label: 6 United States
children.text: The term “United States” includes all territory and waters, continental, or insular, subject to the jurisdiction of the United States.
